name: pytest-xprocess

metadata.name: pytest-xprocess
metadata.summary: A pytest plugin for managing processes across test runs.

documentation:
# pytest-xprocess

A pytest plugin for managing external processes across test runs.

## Installing

Install using [pip]:

```text
pip install pytest-xprocess
```

## Useful Links

- Documentation: <https://pytest-xprocess.readthedocs.io/>
- Changelog: <https://pytest-xprocess.readthedocs.io/en/latest/changes.html>
- PyPI Releases: <https://pypi.org/project/pytest-xprocess/>
- Source Code: <https://github.com/pytest-dev/pytest-xprocess>
- Issue Tracker: <https://github.com/pytest-dev/pytest-xprocess/issues/>
- Pytest IRC channel: ircs://irc.libera.chat:6697/#pytest
- Pytest Discord Channel: <https://discord.gg/k7F2ZFvJV3>

[pip]: https://pip.pypa.io/en/stable/quickstart/

functions.name: xprocess.pytest_xprocess.xprocess
documentation:
yield session-scoped XProcess helper to manage long-running
processes required for testing.

classes.name: xprocess.pytest_xprocess.InterruptionHandler
documentation:
The purpose of this class is exposing the
config object containing references necessary
to properly clean-up in the event of an exception
during test runs

classes.name: xprocess.xprocess.XProcessInfo

methods.name: xprocess.xprocess.XProcessInfo.terminate
documentation:
Recursively terminates process tree.

> Attempt graceful termination starting by leaves of process tree.
>
> A ─┐
>
> : │
>   ├─ B (child) ─┐
>   │ └─ X (grandchild) ─┐
>   │ └─ Y (great grandchild)
>   ├─ C (child)
>   └─ D (child)
>
> 1. kill_list = [A, B, X, Y, C, D]
> 2. reversed(kill_list) = [D, C, Y, X, B, A]
> 3. terminated reversed kill_list

This is the default behavior unless explicitly disabled by setting
kill_proc_tree keyword-only parameter to false when calling
`XProcessInfo.terminate`.

```
param kill_proc_tree

Enable/disable recursive process tree
termination. Defaults to True.

param timeout

Maximum time in seconds to wait on process termination.
When timeout is reached after sending SIGTERM, this
method will attempt to SIGKILL the process and
return -1 in case the operation times out again.
```

return codes:

: 0 no work to do
  1 terminated
  -1 failed to terminate

methods.name: xprocess.xprocess.XProcessInfo.isrunning

documentation:
Holds information of an active process instance represented by
a XProcess Object and offers recursive termination functionality of
said process tree.

classes.name: xprocess.xprocess.XProcessResources
documentation:
Resources used by a running process.
Each time XProcess.ensure is called a single XProcessResources
instance will be created and all resources used by the started
process will be held by it. Namely: file handle, XProcessInfo
and Popen instance.

classes.name: xprocess.xprocess.XProcess

methods.name: xprocess.xprocess.XProcess.getinfo
documentation: Return Process Info for the given external process.

methods.name: xprocess.xprocess.XProcess.ensure

documentation:
Main xprocess class. Represents a running process instance for which
a set of actions is offered, such as process startup, command line actions
and information fetching.

classes.name: xprocess.xprocess.ProcessStarter

methods.name: xprocess.xprocess.ProcessStarter.args
documentation: The args to start the process.

methods.name: xprocess.xprocess.ProcessStarter.pattern
documentation: The pattern to match when the process has started.

methods.name: xprocess.xprocess.ProcessStarter.startup_check
documentation: Used to assert process responsiveness after pattern match

methods.name: xprocess.xprocess.ProcessStarter.wait_callback
documentation:
Assert that process is ready to answer queries using provided
callback funtion. Will raise TimeoutError if self.callback does not
return True before self.timeout seconds

methods.name: xprocess.xprocess.ProcessStarter.wait
documentation: Wait until the pattern is matched or callback returns successful.

methods.name: xprocess.xprocess.ProcessStarter.wait_pattern
documentation: Wait until the pattern is mached and callback returns successful.

methods.name: xprocess.xprocess.ProcessStarter.filter_lines
documentation: fetch first <max_read_lines>, ignoring blank lines.

methods.name: xprocess.xprocess.ProcessStarter.log_line
documentation: Write line to process log file.

methods.name: xprocess.xprocess.ProcessStarter.get_lines
documentation:
Read and yield one line at a time from log_file. Will raise
TimeoutError if pattern is not matched before self.timeout
seconds.
